title: SSCC — Serial Shipping Container Code
objectives: Structure an SSCC (extension digit + GCP + serial + check)

sections.markdown: An **SSCC** (Serial Shipping Container Code) is an 18-digit identifier for a **logistics unit** — a pallet, carton, or roll cage — that travels through the supply chain. Every SSCC is unique; you never reuse it (traceability depends on it).

sections.code:
SSCC structure (18 digits):
  [Extension][GCP][Serial Reference][Check Digit]
  Extension: 0..9 (chosen by the brand — expands capacity)
  Total data: 17 digits + 1 check digit

On a logistics label: SSCC uses Application Identifier (00)
  GS1-128:  (00) 3 8901234 000000001 5

sections.markdown: SSCC vs GTIN: **GTIN identifies a product type; SSCC identifies a specific physical shipment container**. Two identical pallets of the same GTIN each have their own SSCC. That's what enables track-and-trace of shipments.
Reusing an SSCC 'because the shipment is done.' GS1 requires SSCCs remain unique for at least **12 months**. Reusing too soon breaks recalls and reconciliation with historical logistics data.

flashcards.back: A specific logistics unit — pallet, carton or container travelling in the supply chain.
18 digits total, first digit is an 'extension' (0–9).
Do not reuse for at least 12 months — traceability data depends on uniqueness.

options: GTIN = product type; SSCC = physical logistics unit

quiz.explain: Reuse too early breaks recalls and reconciliation.

challenges.text: Compute the check digit for a 17-digit SSCC seed.
Print a GS1-128 label encoding `(00) SSCC` and scan-test it.
Add a per-warehouse SSCC allocator that never repeats (persistent counter).
Model pallet → cartons → items with SSCC/SSCC/SGTIN in EPCIS `aggregation` events.

interviewQuestions: How is SSCC encoded on a logistics label?
